folio.label: 195r
folio.l: The fox answered , in feitú it shal be doon
And as he spak æ word , al sodeynly
The Cok brak from his moutú , delyuerly
And hye vp on a tree , he flaw a noon
And whan the fox saw , æ the Cok was gon
Allas q¶ he , o chaunteclere allas
I haue to ©ow q¶ he , I don trespas
In as meche , as I maked ©ow affer¶
Whan I ©ow hente , & brougút in to this ©er¶
But sithe I dede it , in no wykke entente
Come doun , & I shal telle ©ow what I mente
I shal sey sotú to ©ow , god helpe me so
Nay , tha~ne q¶ he , I shrewe vs bothe two
And first I shrewe my self , bothe blode & bones
If æè begile me , oftere than ones
Thow shalt nomore , thurgú thy flaterie
Do me to synge , & wynke w myn eye
For he æ wynketú , whan he shulde see
Al wilfully , god lat úi~ neuäe thee
Nay q¶ the fox , but god ©eue úi~ meschaunce
That is so vndiscret , of gouänaunce
That iangletú , whan he shulde holde his pees
Lo whicú it is , for to be rechelees
And necligent , and troste on flaterie
But ©e æ holden this tale , of folie
As of a fox , or of a Cok , or hen
Taketú the moralitee , goode men
For Seynt Poule seitú , æ al æat wryten is
To oure docténe , it is I wryte I wys
Taketú the fruyt , & lat the chaf be stille
Now goode god , if æ it be thy wylle
As seitú my lord , so make vs alle goode men
An brynge vs to his blisse,

note.notetxt: 626the folio is copyed with dark brown and also the following link L31.ODR
